judgment.summary: Workday exhibits high gross margins and robust free cash flow alongside significant projected earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS 2.47 vs estimate 2.32 (beat); next earnings date 2026-05-21.
positives: PEG ratio 0.46 — suggests undervaluation relative to growth.
Forward P/E 9.64 vs trailing 46.31 — indicates significant expected earnings expansion.
Gross margin 75.7% — reflects high-quality revenue and strong unit economics.
Free cash flow (TTM) $3.01B — demonstrates strong liquidity generation.
negatives: Profit margin 7.3% — shows relatively thin bottom-line conversion.
Operating margin 12.0% — indicates significant operating expenses relative to gross profit.
Return on assets 3.6% — reflects modest efficiency in asset utilization.

news.paragraph: Workday has recently been recognized as a leader in talent acquisition suites and has expanded its AI offerings through new integrations with Microsoft 365 Copilot. While these product developments are positive, Argus Research has lowered its price target to $131.00, and the stock has recently underperformed the broader market. Options traders should consider this tension between strong product recognition and recent downward analyst adjustments and price weakness.
